title: The African Agenda of Evangelical Christians in Brazilian Foreign Policy: The Crisis of the Universal Church of the Kingdom of God in Angola
qualitative_goal_quote: this exploratory work aimed to map the discourse and incidence of Evangelical actors in Brazilian foreign policy during the Jair Bolsonaro administration, based on the case study of the most influential Brazilian Neo-Pentecostal institution, the UCKG
causal_or_explanatory_claim_quote: Brazil’s African policy has not been abandoned, but rather changed in favor of the only government support group with stronger interests on the African continent: transnational Evangelical missionary groups
main_variables_or_relationship: The article analyzes how evangelical and Neo-Pentecostal actors, especially the UCKG, influenced Brazilian foreign policy toward Africa during the Bolsonaro administration, with the UCKG crisis in Angola as the main case.
sample_or_data_source: Qualitative case study using secondary academic literature, public speeches, news reporting, diplomatic episodes, institutional histories, and public statements concerning Bolsonaro-era foreign policy and the UCKG in Angola.
brief_justification: The article is an empirical qualitative case study. It reconstructs evangelical influence on Bolsonaro-era foreign policy and the UCKG crisis in Angola using documentary, journalistic, historical, and secondary-source evidence. Contextual numbers appear, but there is no original quantitative analysis, statistical inference, or credibility-revolution causal design.
